programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to the bayou to track down a cagey alligator in order to stop a deadly mosquito-borne virus. Paige teaches Walter the concept of a 'white lie'.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team use their high IQs to win a softball game when they are challenged by the homeland security director, with all their future government contracts on the line.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty confronts Alexa Comescu and tells her Callen is dead, but the crime boss knows she is lying. Real-time episode of the crime drama, starring Linda Hunt, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue

programme.title: Bondi Rescue

However, the team was still around to notice and rescue a swimmer in fatal danger. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: It's an emotional time for Terry as he starts searching for missing nephew Bradley, and when faithful colleague Ramani provides much-needed support, he finally begins to show his true feelings for her. However, the beleaguered copper is soon facing further problems when Bradley turns up and tells his father about the relationship between his mother and Terry. Meanwhile, Will tries to clear the air with Honey over making a move so soon after Scott's death - and when Amber gets wind that Honey's not interested in the hunky new PC, she goes in for the kill.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Amber's bad attitude and generally poor performance put her on the wrong side of Gina, who tells the flighty young PC she wants her out of the force. But Amber challenges her inspector, claiming she knows her rights and has the law on her side. Smithy is surprised to receive a call from ex-lover and gangster's wife Louise Larson, and says he wants nothing to do with her. But when he discovers she is testifying against her husband and going into witness protection, he is given plenty of food for thought. Jo is amused by Suzie's 'by-the-book' approach to her sergeant exams and Tony considers moonlighting as a cabbie to help raise money for his father.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy tells Gina he's considering leaving the job and joining Louise in witness protection, much to his inspector's horror. Later, when he visits his former lover to talk, it's not long before they fall back into bed. Meanwhile, it's a bad day for Amber - having suffered a roasting from Gina, she now faces a pep talk from Yvonne, and ends up placing them both in jeopardy. Eventually, the penny drops for the impulsive young PC, who decides the police service isn't for her and hands in her resignation - but not before telling her colleagues exactly what she thinks of them. Mickey covers for Suzie when she makes an error of judgement, and realising she's exhausted from all her studying, he offers to help her revise.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack Meadows' world starts to fall apart when a dying convict accuses him of a frame-up more than 20 years ago and DI Neil Manson is brought in to investigate his old boss. To make matters worse, suspicion falls on his drug-addicted son Benjamin for a series of robberies, and wife Laura blames him for getting the wayward lad locked up. As the evidence stacks up against him, Jack finds there's nowhere to turn.(n)

programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars.(n)

programme.desc: Period drama about a 19-year-old woman, who is attacked after an engagement party in honour of a farmer for whom she has feelings. Carli Norris stars.(n)

programme.desc: Following the destruction of their home by the McGraths, Tilly and her grandmother are forced to live a life of solitude. Period drama, starring Carli Norris.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Harry notices that Sarah is being followed, so Roy questions Gordon when he finds him waiting outside the hospital. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.desc: Carolyn and Jack visit Douglas on their honeymoon and learn that Sir Richard is up to his dirty tricks in persuading donors to cease funding the refuge for retired servicemen. programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard finds time weighing heavily on his hands, so Hyacinth decides to provide him with a hobby - a move that backfires on her in spectacular fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.desc: Hyacinth develops a passion for art, Rose acquires a new admirer, and Daddy has a yearning to join the Foreign Legion. Patricia Routledge stars.(n)

programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey.(n)

programme.desc: The discovery of a bloodstained pair of hot pants results in the team being asked to reinvestigate the 1971 disappearance of a mother and her six-month-old son. Visiting a local leather tannery with Jack, he criticises its health and safety record, losing the practice a valuable contract _ then goes back to apologise and makes matters even worse.(n)

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul faces a complex moral dilemma when a body found in a field turns out to be that of a peeping Tom who had videotaped a murder. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey's kitchen goes up in smoke so Dorien, looking for an escape from visiting her mother-in-law, allows the sisters spend the weekend with her while the new kitchen is installed. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad is ordered by its new boss to look into the unsolved murder of a barrister found bound and gagged in his car 25 years ago. The case was a rare failure for a former chief inspector, although his notes point to a very clear suspect. As they uncover the late advocate's unusual hobbies, they gradually learn exactly what happened. Anita Dobson, Jenny Agutter and Keith Barron guest star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An armed police raid at a terraced house in North London sees two suspected terrorists killed, along with a police officer. Nikki doubts the officers' accounts of events after carrying out the post mortems as the forensic evidence just doesn't add up. The case then takes an unexpected turn when it transpires one of the surviving policemen is having an affair with his dead colleague's wife.(n)

programme.desc: Fflur is about to move into the hospice, but both she and Dylan struggle with the decision. A face from the past threatens Vince's plans to sell a table from the school storehouse.(n)

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather.(n)

programme.title: Cefn Gwlad
programme.desc: Mari Lovgreen presents clips of Dai Llanilar's foreign trips. Plus, a visit to a wood chipping factory in Llanrwst.(n)

programme.title: Tewach Na Dwr
programme.desc: A woman living on an idyllic island is reunited with her adult children, who are forced to come to terms with old memories, resentments and secrets.(n)

programme.title: Bad Achub Porthdinllaen
programme.desc: Documentary following the volunteers who work for the Royal National Lifeboat Institution on the Llyn Peninsula and are willing to put their lives on the line each time they receive a call for help. During a year of change and exciting developments, they move to a new home and take on a new boat.(n)

programme.title: Y Tywydd
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Diwedd
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler plants root crops and leafy vegetables in her urban garden, including kale, Swiss chard and beetroot, before preparing dishes of rhubarb and raspberry pie, and beetroot and yoghurt soup. She also collects a good supply of eggs from her two chickens.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Northamptonshire to help create a low-allergen garden for a couple whose young son is affected by severe allergies.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As organisations like the National Trust open up to visitors again, Ellie Harrison heads to Charlecote Park in Warwickshire, one of the great estates of the Elizabethan era. She's rolling up her sleeves to help with Britain's oldest managed flock of Jacob sheep, and visits a working Georgian water mill that's busier than ever, supplying the lockdown baking boom. Dwayne Fields investigates the challenges facing members of the BAME community living in the countryside, and Adam Henson is judging entrants in an online livestock show.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don offers tips on maintaining the vegetable plot for continuous crops. From her garden in Devon, Carol Klein gives a comprehensive guide to getting plants for free, turning her attention to climbers like honeysuckle and clematis. In Brighton, cameras return to Kate Bradbury's garden to see how the once barren plot is being used by wildlife. Plus, from the archives, Rachel de Thame's visit to Gloucestershire's Kiftsgate Court Gardens, where she heard about the three generations of female gardeners who have made their mark there.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson keeps an eye on his cuttings and Kirsty Wilson is extra careful as she shows how to propagate cacti. There is good news and bad news on an allotment in Aberdeen.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary tour of the country, laced with history, literature and stunning photography. Driving a clapped-out old VW camper van around the Galicia region on the Atlantic coast, Rick discovers places off the beaten track as he samples classic dishes of rural Spain, a country he believes has been underrated in cookery circles until quite recently.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Slovakia, Hungary and Romania mark the halfway point in Si King and Dave Myers' baking odyssey. They begin in a cafe in Bratislava, where they are served three cakes inspired by British TV cookery shows, then taste kremesh - the Hungarian version of vanilla slice. They also try a roadside snack of deep-fried pastry with sour cream and cheese, an Eastern European take on bread-and-butter pudding, and cheese and bacon savoury scones, before ending this leg of their journey with a family party thrown by Dave's Romanian wife Lil.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A look at research into the lives of the marine mammals, which were long regarded as fearsome predators until the first one was brought into captivity for study in 1964, revealing facts about their profound intelligence, social interactions and parental instincts. The programme illustrates how contemporary studies continue to unearth revelations about the whales' behaviour.(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. Gareth collaborates with the nation's key workers on the second stage of his project, discovering stories of unsung heroism and uplifting optimism.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie.(n)

programme.title: Ice Station Zebra (1968)
programme.desc: An American atomic submarine commander races against time to reach a Soviet spy satellite that has crash-landed near a British research station in the Arctic - before Russian troops arrive to stake their claim to it. Adventure based on Alistair MacLean's novel, starring Rock Hudson, Ernest Borgnine, Jim Brown, Tony Bill and Patrick McGoohan.(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the North coast of Kent, new RNLI crew member Ruth is on board for one of her first shouts - a member of the public has spotted what looks like an upturned boat out at sea. Off the Essex coast, the crew of West Mersea station launch to reports of a life raft in the North Sea. And at Clacton, the crew get a call in the middle of the night to look for two people in the sea.(n)

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the country as 50 years of civil war draws to a close in the wake of a recently signed peace deal. He meets guerrilla fighters who have pledged to lay down arms, but also discovers a negative consequence of their disarming, as the paramilitary gangs that control the drugs trade grow in power. Simon travels through the country with coca farmers, who discuss the government support they will need in the turbulent times ahead, as poverty and land ownership becoming pressing issues.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of the show's most passionate contestants, including a health food entrepreneur who confused the panel with the precise composition of his low carb noodles, a couple who withhold a vital piece of information while pitching their iced tea company, and a former boxer-turned-inventor.(n)

programme.title: Alex Brooker: Disability and Me
programme.desc: The Last Leg presenter confronts what disability really means in Britain today. Alex is reunited with a childhood friend who has spina bifida, who challenges him to consider the impact of making jokes about his own disability. He also pays a visit to a bespoke disability helpline, meets members of Arsenal's disabled supporters club, and talks to Paralympic swimmer Susie Rodgers about her life.(n)

programme.title: The Kemps: All True
programme.desc: Spoof documentary, starring Gary and Martin Kemp as themselves, following the brothers as they launch new careers in the wake of Spandau Ballet's 40th anniversary celebrations. While Martin tries to get a sci-fi movie franchise off the ground, Gary has a new brand of vegan meat substitute to promote.(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Roland Rat looks back at the year when he was credited with rescuing ailing breakfast station TV-am from ratings collapse, Jane Fonda's workout routines took the world by storm and David Hasselhoff teamed up with supercar KITT in Knight Rider. The student population went head to head in Bob Holness's quiz Blockbusters, while on the music scene Club Tropicana by Wham! topped the charts and Michael Jackson's celebrated Thriller video was aired for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the biggest hits of the year, celebrating the genres of soul, reggae, jazz, new wave and pop. Wham!, KC and the Sunshine Band, the Police, Culture Club, Siouxsie and the Banshees, UB40, Duran Duran, the Beat and Bananarama feature, alongside Elton John, Bonnie Tyler and Malcolm McLaren's Double Dutch.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Lily Rice, the first British person to land a wheelchair backflip, and how her life was transformed when the footage of her went viral.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: It's a busy weekend for the medics as thousands of visitors flood into the city for the Grand National weekend - and first up is a visitor falling down some steps at the train station. Mo and Tony are called to a baby that has fallen from its pram, and soon realise the mother is under the influence of alcohol, while Anthony and Mandy are dispatched to a nursing home where a patient is confused state fainting. Paramedics and sisters Danielle and Stacey treat the first casualty at the racecourse - a jockey who has broken his ankle falling from his horse, meaning he will have to miss the big race the following day.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is devastated after a bad performance review. He is tempted to cross an ethical line to turn things around, but is unprepared for the terrible consequences of his actions. Nicky tries to find a life-saving treatment for Brenda, which puts him at odds with Jac and forced to choose between her professional and personal priorities. Fletch recovers from surgery, and Essie hides the pain from recent events involving Isla.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson recalls a selection of the greatest Escape to the Country house-hunts featuring buyers hoping to earn a living from their new country home. Whether seeking a farmhouse with established holiday lets or some land with which to earn from a potential glamping enterprise, the properties show that rural businesses can help pay the bills.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Ian Humphries, Jane Cave, Adrian Higham, James Gooch, Natasha Francis and Tracy Martin go head to head over a polychrome carving of St Sebastian, a pair of vintage Bakelite phones, some Chinese fortune sticks and a piece of rock 'n' roll history. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: On the storm-lashed Cornish coastline, a pair of shags struggle to rear their chicks, while a young female pine marten prepares to rear her young on a remote Scottish peninsular. Plus, there's a moment of magic as the team celebrates the epic beauty of Dartmoor. Presented by Chris Packham, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Paul Rose, Tooni Mahto and Frank Pope conduct an exploration of marine life in the North Sea, and how it has been affected by the construction of a wind farm off the Norfolk coast. They also observe England's largest colony of grey seals in the Farne Islands, find out why Bridlington, East Yorkshire, has enjoyed a particularly bountiful lobster harvest and learn more about how fish behave when threatened by predators.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen arrive in the Roman city of Bath, where they seek out the finest delicacies, from beetroot sorbet to honey glazed dormice. A reviving brew served by the UK's reigning king of the baristas sets them up for a visit to the catacombs where an eco food initiative is making mushrooms out of waste cardboard and coffee grounds.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Evonne Goolagong Cawley v Chris Evert and Bjorn Borg v John McEnroe. Clare Balding presents action from the 1980 singles finals at the All England Club.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, a place bursting with remnants from the town's industrial past. Elizabeth Talbot and James Lewis find the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction from the assembled crowd, while Paul visits the home of the man now considered to be the father of ecology, 18th-century naturalist Gilbert White.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman focuses on memorable conversions featured on the programme, from mills and schools to pubs and chapels, and even an old slaughterhouse.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Pub landlord Al Murray is locked down and locked in, while Richard Herring challenges himself to a tense game of snooker and Jayde Adams moans about living underneath fitness guru Joe Wicks. Phil Wang tries to tidy up his falt in the style of Marie Kondo style, and Vic Reeves performs in his garden.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts an edition of the comedy quiz show. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians James Acaster, Tom Allen, Ari Eldjarn, Ed Gamble and Ellie Taylor. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Italy's Frontline: A Doctor's Diary
programme.desc: Documentary featuring an unprecedented and intimate insight into three months of life on the Covid frontline in Italy with A&E doctor Francesca Mangiatordi. In the early days of the outbreak, the hospital is overflowing and Francesa works gruelling 12-hour shifts caring for those who have been most affected. Due to a lack of life-saving equipment she faces the traumatising task of prioritising which lives to try to save. At home, Francesca is fearful of infecting her husband, who suffers from a respiratory condition, and she hasn't touched him - or her children - for more than a month. Narrated by Florence Pugh.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As organisations like the National Trust open up to visitors again, Ellie Harrison heads to Charlecote Park in Warwickshire, one of the great estates of the Elizabethan era. She's rolling up her sleeves to help with Britain's oldest managed flock of Jacob sheep, and visits a working Georgian water mill that's busier than ever, supplying the lockdown baking boom. Dwayne Fields investigates the challenges facing members of the BAME community living in the countryside, and Adam Henson is judging entrants in an online livestock show.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Fabie, a newly qualified nurse, struggles to cope with life in one of the busiest A&E departments, so she is moved to the surgical assessment unit to protect her mental wellbeing.(n)

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates a spin-off show delving deeper into three repairs to show how the heirlooms help shaped modern life, beginning with the story of toys.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman focuses on memorable conversions featured on the programme, from mills and schools to pubs and chapels, and even an old slaughterhouse.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Paul Rose, Tooni Mahto and Frank Pope assist with the excavation of a 17th-century ship discovered in the waters outside Poole Harbour, Dorset, which could be one of the most important finds in British maritime history. They also learn about the plight of spiny seahorses in Dorset's Studland Bay, explore marine life beneath Brighton Pier and examine the wreck of an experimental Second World War submarine.(n)

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Mason McQueen's black cab takes Terry Wogan to the Victorian spa town of Harrogate in North Yorkshire, where they enjoy a Turkish bath and drink inordinate amounts of tea. They also sample a toffee that is actually a barley sugar, visit Agatha Christie's hide-out hotel and enjoy a slap-up dinner - with Yorkshire pudding for desert.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents a look back at two classic finals from the All England Club - Billie Jean King v Ann Jones from 1969 and Roger Federer v Andy Roddick from 2009.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Mark Stacey search through the items brought to the RAF Museum in Hendon, London, and Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson brings together some of the show's greatest properties in Norfolk, including a 17th-century thatched cottage and a 400-year-old listed manor house.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. Gareth employs technology to bring the voices of performers from across the country together online even if social distancing makes large gatherings impossible. The final episode features the country's most vulnerable people, who have contributed to the project while self-shielding, and discovers their personal stories of finding hope during isolation.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Priest School
programme.desc: Rare access to the inner workings, personnel, seminarians and history of the oldest Scottish institution abroad - Il Pontificio College Scozzese; The Scots College in Rome. Narrated by Daniela Nardini.(n)

programme.title: The Hidden Wilds of the Motorway
programme.desc: Author and naturalist Helen Macdonald embarks on a clockwise loop around London's orbital motorway - searching for wildness and natural beauty within sight and sound of the M25. Helen encounters the remarkable people, plants and animals living above, beside and beneath the motorway, and delves into the controversial history of the UK's longest bypass.(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground.(n)

programme.title: Much Ado About Nothing: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Autumn 1918, and a group of soldiers return from the trenches. The world-weary Benedick and his friend Claudio find themselves reacquainted with Beatrice and Hero. As memories of conflict give way to a life of parties and masked balls, Claudio and Hero fall deeply in love, while Benedick and Beatrice reignite their own altogether more combative courtship. Christopher Luscombe directs Michelle Terry and Edward Bennett as Beatrice and Benedick in this acclaimed RSC production of the Bard's classic comedy.(n)

programme.title: Knights of Classic Drama at the BBC
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archive footage of some of Britain's most acclaimed acting legends appearing in BBC drama at early stages of their careers. The programme features early roles and some career-defining moments for Judi Dench, Maggie Smith, Diana Rigg and Helen Mirren. Narrated by David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Apples, Pears and Paint: How to Make a Still Life Painting
programme.desc: Examining the history of the art form that depicts fruit, flowers and domestic objects, from the Xenia murals at Pompeii through to the cubist works of Picasso. The film also takes a look at the work of some of the artists synonymous with the genre, including Chardin and Cezanne, and features contributions from Bettany Hughes, Janina Ramirez and Andrew Graham-Dixon.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca has big plans for her kids' return, Steven pushes for a closer relationship with Stacey, with embarrassing consequences, and Jack and Ronnie grow closer.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey changes her mind about Steven, Bianca is determined to win her kids round, and Ronnie feels pushed out by the arrival of Penny.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Steven feels under pressure on his first night with Stacey, Ronnie and Roxy's rivalry escalates over the barmaid of the year competition, and Sean has a nasty surprise for Gus.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Christian tries to get the bottom of Steven's angst. Roxy and Dawn slug it out in the barmaid of the year competition. Pat finds out the truth about Ricky's situation.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a water scene offset by an abandoned shipping boat hugging a pier and some charming old dockside storage sheds.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga's exploration of the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa concludes. This time, he profiles the three African kings who took a stand against the machinations of an empire, along with a crooner whose voice was considered 'irresistible'. He also investigates a history of race riots in Liverpool, and outlines how the sense of a black British identity has been shaped by various forces throughout the span of the 20th century.(n)

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: A profile of Ludwig van Beethoven to mark the 250th anniversary of his birth, beginning by exploring his childhood, and his subsequent rise to fame as a piano virtuoso, then composer, in late 18th-century Vienna. Featuring Mark Padmore singing one of Beethoven's early lieder Adelaide, and conductor Marin Alsop talking about the premiere of the Ninth Symphony.(n)

programme.title: The Long Song
programme.desc: In 19th-century Jamaica, July, a strong-willed slave tries to survive at the Amity sugar cane plantation owned by her odious mistress, Caroline Mortimer. However, the Christmas riots and the abolition of slavery turn the world of Amity upside down and throw July's destiny into question, but when order is restored and new overseer Robert Goodwin arrives, July and Caroline are both intrigued by his seemingly revolutionary plans to improve the plantation. Drama based on the novel by Andrea Levy, starring Tamara Lawrance, Hayley Atwell and Jack Lowden.(n)

programme.title: The Long Song
programme.desc: Robert's arrival at Amity plantation complicates things as both Caroline and July compete for his attention, but he is more preoccupied with re-organising the plantation along more humane lines. Later, the harvest is ready to be taken in, but tensions rise between the workers and Robert as he expects the workers to put in long hours, but they now feel they can challenge his authority.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby examines artefacts that reflect Britain's imperialist history during the 19th century, including a George IV jigsaw of the world and paintings of Cook's voyages. He travels to India to view buildings such as Government House and Howrah Station in Calcutta, which demonstrate the grandeur of the Raj, and studies items that show the darker side of empire-building.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: This profile, featuring an audio interview with Cindy Sherman, gives a rare insight into one of the most elusive and influential living artists. Contributors include Kim Cattrall and Gaby Hoffmann.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a water scene offset by an abandoned shipping boat hugging a pier and some charming old dockside storage sheds.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect.(n)

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: A profile of Ludwig van Beethoven to mark the 250th anniversary of his birth, beginning by exploring his childhood, and his subsequent rise to fame as a piano virtuoso, then composer, in late 18th-century Vienna. Featuring Mark Padmore singing one of Beethoven's early lieder Adelaide, and conductor Marin Alsop talking about the premiere of the Ninth Symphony.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates soft purple sky and tall trees that almost seem to float in the hazy foothills of a distant, blue-grey mountain.(n)

programme.title: Pavlopetri - The City Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Archaeologists use submersible robots and computer simulations to investigate the submerged city of Pavlopetri, which lies off the southern coast of the Greek mainland. Originally rediscovered in 1967, it is believed to be the oldest underwater city in the world. Philip Glenister narrates this documentary following Jon Henderson of the University of Nottingham as he leads a team of experts hoping to discover what life was like for the citizens of Pavlopetri 3,500 years ago.(n)

programme.title: Black Nurses: The Women Who Saved the NHS
programme.desc: The story of the thousands of Caribbean and African women who answered the call 70 years ago to come to the UK and help build the National Health Service. Told by the women themselves, the documentary reveals the price they paid in leaving behind their families, and examines their struggle to overcome racism and their fight for career progression, as well as their cultural impact here and abroad.(n)

programme.title: The Long Song
programme.desc: Robert is increasingly angry and frustrated by the workers' refusal to follow his orders, so he takes violent revenge on them - much to the shock and horror of July. With the crop starting to spoil and Amity in ruins, his sanity begins to unravel - with possibly devastating consequences for those closest to him. Drama, starring Tamara Lawrance and Jack Lowden.(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the novelist Andrea Levy, charting her journey to become one of the country's best loved contemporary novelists. He looks back at her best-selling novel, Small Island, which captured the imagination of readers around the world, and her most recent novel, The Long Song, has recently been adapted by the BBC, featuring an all-star cast.(n)

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of
programme.desc: In episode four of Show Me What You're Made Of our teens they leave Cambodia to fly further east to the island of Taiwan. Here they head straight to an enormous tea plantation where they grow the tea for the British export market. It's been a tough trip for our five teens and within minutes of arriving at the tea factory one of them feels unwell. With a man down how will the other four cope with the very physical work involved? Their boss is the strictest one yet and he is not a man to be messed with. At the end of their first shift the boss proves that he is very serious about tea when he sacks one of the team. They may be shocked but can they pull together as a team to turn this situation around? Show Me What You're Made Of is presented by Stacey Dooley.(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Community on Solid Ground is a youth centre in Whalley Range. Before its current management took over the building the area didn't offer much for the youngsters living there and anti-social behaviour was very common. That has dropped by 73 per cent since Community on Solid Ground came along, the kids can hang out, play sports and have a place to go that means they aren't hanging around the streets. Sadly the facilities are very tired and they don't have areas they can play their beloved sport on, so the Dengineers are going to transform their outside space into a sports arena worthy of the Olympics. Presented by Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield.(n)

At the ranch Matilda and her brother Jack, mum Tana and dad Gordon saddle up on horseback, learn how to ride horses like cowboys and get the chance to round up cattle - Wild West style. Wolves and Arsenal drew 1-1 in November's reverse fixture, but both sides will have been looking for three points on this occasion to boost their chances of playing European football next season. Meanwhile, Leicester will have hoped to continue what has been an excellent season so far for them by completing a double over the Eagles. Plus, Norwich City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Manchester United v Bournemouth and Chelsea v Watford.(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by shadow chancellor Anneliese Dodds, NHS England chief executive Simon Stevens and Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist and national editor of the Washington Post Mary Jordan. The newspapers are reviewed by Helen Pidd and Vicki Young.(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams.(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper advise teams at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, and Eric Knowles finds out about an ancient ferry service due to be reinstate.(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering the truth about football in Wales. It's a story of weekend warriors, boggy pitches and dodgy tackles, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past.(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in central London to mark the birthday of the NHS and Florence Nightingale's bicentenary, and the Archbishop of Canterbury pays tribute to frontline carers.(n)

programme.title: Andy Murray's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Sue Barker concludes her look at the career of the British tennis star, including a 12-month spell from late-2015 that saw him win the Davis Cup, Wimbledon and the Olympics.(n)

programme.title: Arthur Ashe: More Than a Champion
programme.desc: Forty years after Arthur Ashe became the first black man to win the Wimbledon singles championship, this documentary tells his story, from his boyhood in segregated America to his sporting career and his later life as a campaigner on issues such as Aids and HIV, and the mistreatment of refugees. Serena Williams, Martina Navratilova, John McEnroe, Ilie Nastase and Andy Murray explain Ashe's legacy and their own personal debt to him, while there are also contributions from his brother Johnnie and friends Stan Smith and Donald Dell.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker explores the stretch of the Grand Union Canal that runs close to his home in the Chilterns - meeting those who live and work on the waterway and also getting to grips with an engineering marvel that keeps the whole system flowing. Elsewhere, Tom Heap investigates how farmers are adjusting to the challenges posed by climate change, and John Craven launches this year's Countryfile photography competition with the help of singer and broadcaster Cerys Matthews and wildlife expert Simon King.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Southampton v Manchester City (Kick-off 7.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Premier League fixture, which takes place at St Mary's Stadium. The visitors will be looking to improve upon their excellent recent record against the Saints, which currently stands at seven consecutive wins in all competitions. Last season's corresponding fixture finished 3-1, with strikes from David Silva, Sergio Aguero and a James Ward-Prowse own goal handing Pep Guardiola's men all three points.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: In 1865, Emery returns to Hokitika and reunites with Anna, now heavily pregnant and addicted to opium. Carver threatens her in order to force Emery to reveal where Crosbie Wells is - with disastrous consequences. In 1866, Lydia goes about framing Anna for Crosbie's murder, while others in the town are beginning to turn against Anna, who fears that Emery is dead and that she is going insane. When chemist Joseph Pritchard confronts her about her involvement in Crosbie's death, she takes matters into her own hands.(n)

programme.title: Just Call Me Martina
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents a profile of former tennis star Martina Navratilova, documenting her achievements on the court in a professional career that spanned more than 30 years and saw her win Grand Slam titles in four different decades. Martina also goes back to her home town of Revnice in the Czech Republic, and allows the cameras to film her wedding in New York and the celebrations in Miami. Featuring contributions by Chris Ever, Elton John, Stephen Fry, Billie Jean King, Novak Djokovic, Rafael Nadal, Pam Shriver and Rod Laver.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Covid-19 has killed thousands, but now there are fears the pandemic has caused a crisis in cancer care that could mean many thousands more will die prematurely. Reporter Deborah James, who herself has incurable bowel cancer, explores the impact of the focus on coronavirus on cancer treatment. She investigates how the NHS has managed care during lockdown, speaks to experts and analyses new research, and also meets fellow patients to discover what the pandemic has meant for them.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by James Bye and Davood Ghadami, aka market regulars Martin Fowler and Kush Kazemi, who talk about their time working on the soap. The pair look back at their earliest scenes and biggest storylines yet - from a dramatic love triangle with Stacey Slater and the Kazemi family's hard-hitting brush with knife crime, as well as Martin's more recent journey to Albert Square bad boy.(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Though aged in his 70s, the rugby legend is busier than ever, so wife Maureen persuades him to take life a little easier by exploring the four Welsh canals. The duo take charge of a narrowboat on the Llangollen Canal, but Gareth's stress levels go soon through the roof as he tries to avoid hitting obstacles. Somehow, Wales' much-loved couple still find time to appreciate the breath-taking scenery.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Australian psychological thriller, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw as two women hiding secrets and whose worlds are about to collide. Blogger Meghan is currently expecting her third child, but fears for the impact it will have on her already failing marriage to ambitious sports reporter Jack. Among her devoted fans is struggling supermarket employee Agatha, who appears to be heavily pregnant herself - but not everything is as it appears.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Comedy in which actors David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown. The Good Omens co-stars were due to put on a play in the West End before the production was put on hold because of the coronavirus pandemic. Not wanting to let his big break pass him by, director Simon Evans must do his best to convince his doubtful actors to carry on rehearsals online.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella's bloated social media presence finds her more glued to her phone than ever, perpetually internalising the stress of her followers. At a Halloween 'paint and wine' party organised by Terry, Kwame opens up to the girls about his recent sexual experimentation - and Arabella's reaction lands her in an emergency session with Carrie. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, written by and starring Michaela Coel, with Paapa Essiedu, Weruche Opia and Andi Osho.(n)

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Elisha Cuthbert presents as 12 drag queens showcase their charisma, uniqueness, nerve and talent in a bid to become Canada's First Drag Superstar and to secure a $100,000 prize. Their efforts are assessed by a judging panel comprising drag queen and ballet dancer Brooke Lynn Hytes, actor and fashion model Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and fashion model and runway coach Stacey McKenzie.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Aisling Bea and Stephen Graham pop by for a chat, Peter takes Joe Cole on an extreme retirement challenge, and John Legend Saves Our Summer with a live performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Amira and Syed's wedding arrives in a classic episode from 2010. Zainab questions Christian about his behaviour toward her son - leading to a shocking revelation. Masood is furious to learn Inzamam made a pass at his wife and hands his brother his money back, but luckily Qadim changes his mind and decides to pay for his daughter's big day after all.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nicky is stressed and exhausted, with devastating consequences at work. As she struggles to cope with the pressure from her mother, a tragic incident pushes her over the edge. Ange tells Cameron he is not ready to take the next step in his career. Frustrated by the rejection, he takes out his anger on Louis. Sacha decides to respect Essie's wishes concerning her cancer treatment, but Dominic urges him to persuade her not to give up on palliative care.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Agatha's fake pregnancy is nearly exposed when she is taken into police custody, and she is forced to change her plans when Meghan's due date is brought forward. Jack's best friend Simon begins to suspect that he is the real father of Meghan's baby.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: With rehearsals due to begin, director Simon goes missing. Michael and David embrace the opportunity to skive off from their duties, but one of them gets caught breaking the law. Georgia is due to deliver her novel to her agent and asks David to stand in and help with the kids.(n)

Their efforts are assessed by a judging panel comprising drag queen and ballet dancer Brooke Lynn Hytes, actor and fashion model Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and fashion model and runway coach Stacey McKenzie.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Aisling Bea and Stephen Graham pop by for a chat, Peter takes Joe Cole on an extreme retirement challenge, and John Legend Saves Our Summer with a live performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Amira and Syed's wedding arrives in a classic episode from 2010. Zainab questions Christian about his behaviour toward her son - leading to a shocking revelation. Masood is furious to learn Inzamam made a pass at his wife and hands his brother his money back, but luckily Qadim changes his mind and decides to pay for his daughter's big day after all.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nicky is stressed and exhausted, with devastating consequences at work. As she struggles to cope with the pressure from her mother, a tragic incident pushes her over the edge. Ange tells Cameron he is not ready to take the next step in his career. Frustrated by the rejection, he takes out his anger on Louis. Sacha decides to respect Essie's wishes concerning her cancer treatment, but Dominic urges him to persuade her not to give up on palliative care.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Agatha's fake pregnancy is nearly exposed when she is taken into police custody, and she is forced to change her plans when Meghan's due date is brought forward. Jack's best friend Simon begins to suspect that he is the real father of Meghan's baby.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: With rehearsals due to begin, director Simon goes missing. Michael and David embrace the opportunity to skive off from their duties, but one of them gets caught breaking the law. Georgia is due to deliver her novel to her agent and asks David to stand in and help with the kids.(n)

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle hosts a talk show from her own home, in which she and her guests offer lifestyle advice for coping with the struggles of isolation. In the first episode, Michelle is joined remotely by Jade Thirlwall from Little Mix, gets a health check from Dr Ranj Singh, and shares hair care tips with Susannah Constantine.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Advanced paramedic Kirsten is faced with an unusual job. A call has come in from police about a burglar who has injured himself trying to escape from the house he was looting. As she arrives, she finds him in agony with a nasty open fracture on his leg. Later, Kelsey and Dave are called to a pregnant woman in a lot of pain who worries she may be in early labour, and Gareth and Gabby treat a woman who is struggling with her breathing. But then two high-priority calls come in - both of which involve children.(n)

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Wolverhampton Wanderers v Arsenal and Leicester City v Crystal Palace. Wolves and Arsenal drew 1-1 in November's reverse fixture, but both sides will have been looking for three points on this occasion to boost their chances of playing European football next season. Meanwhile, Leicester will have hoped to continue what has been an excellent season so far for them by completing a double over the Eagles. Plus, Norwich City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Manchester United v Bournemouth and Chelsea v Watford.(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by shadow chancellor Anneliese Dodds, NHS England chief executive Simon Stevens and Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist and national editor of the Washington Post Mary Jordan. The newspapers are reviewed by Helen Pidd and Vicki Young.(n)

programme.title: Andy Murray's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Sue Barker concludes her look at the career of the British tennis star, including a 12-month spell from late-2015 that saw him win the Davis Cup, Wimbledon and the Olympics.(n)

programme.title: Arthur Ashe: More Than a Champion
programme.desc: Forty years after Arthur Ashe became the first black man to win the Wimbledon singles championship, this documentary tells his story, from his boyhood in segregated America to his sporting career and his later life as a campaigner on issues such as Aids and HIV, and the mistreatment of refugees. Serena Williams, Martina Navratilova, John McEnroe, Ilie Nastase and Andy Murray explain Ashe's legacy and their own personal debt to him, while there are also contributions from his brother Johnnie and friends Stan Smith and Donald Dell.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker explores the stretch of the Grand Union Canal that runs close to his home in the Chilterns - meeting those who live and work on the waterway and also getting to grips with an engineering marvel that keeps the whole system flowing. Elsewhere, Tom Heap investigates how farmers are adjusting to the challenges posed by climate change, and John Craven launches this year's Countryfile photography competition with the help of singer and broadcaster Cerys Matthews and wildlife expert Simon King.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Southampton v Manchester City (Kick-off 7.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Premier League fixture, which takes place at St Mary's Stadium. The visitors will be looking to improve upon their excellent recent record against the Saints, which currently stands at seven consecutive wins in all competitions. Last season's corresponding fixture finished 3-1, with strikes from David Silva, Sergio Aguero and a James Ward-Prowse own goal handing Pep Guardiola's men all three points.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: In 1865, Emery returns to Hokitika and reunites with Anna, now heavily pregnant and addicted to opium. Carver threatens her in order to force Emery to reveal where Crosbie Wells is - with disastrous consequences. In 1866, Lydia goes about framing Anna for Crosbie's murder, while others in the town are beginning to turn against Anna, who fears that Emery is dead and that she is going insane. When chemist Joseph Pritchard confronts her about her involvement in Crosbie's death, she takes matters into her own hands.(n)

programme.title: Just Call Me Martina
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents a profile of former tennis star Martina Navratilova, documenting her achievements on the court in a professional career that spanned more than 30 years and saw her win Grand Slam titles in four different decades. Martina also goes back to her home town of Revnice in the Czech Republic, and allows the cameras to film her wedding in New York and the celebrations in Miami. Featuring contributions by Chris Ever, Elton John, Stephen Fry, Billie Jean King, Novak Djokovic, Rafael Nadal, Pam Shriver and Rod Laver.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Covid-19 has killed thousands, but now there are fears the pandemic has caused a crisis in cancer care that could mean many thousands more will die prematurely. Reporter Deborah James, who herself has incurable bowel cancer, explores the impact of the focus on coronavirus on cancer treatment. She investigates how the NHS has managed care during lockdown, speaks to experts and analyses new research, and also meets fellow patients to discover what the pandemic has meant for them.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by James Bye and Davood Ghadami, aka market regulars Martin Fowler and Kush Kazemi, who talk about their time working on the soap. The pair look back at their earliest scenes and biggest storylines yet - from a dramatic love triangle with Stacey Slater and the Kazemi family's hard-hitting brush with knife crime, as well as Martin's more recent journey to Albert Square bad boy.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Secrets She Keeps
programme.desc: Australian psychological thriller, starring Laura Carmichael and Jessica De Gouw as two women hiding secrets and whose worlds are about to collide. Blogger Meghan is currently expecting her third child, but fears for the impact it will have on her already failing marriage to ambitious sports reporter Jack. Among her devoted fans is struggling supermarket employee Agatha, who appears to be heavily pregnant herself - but not everything is as it appears.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Comedy in which actors David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown. The Good Omens co-stars were due to put on a play in the West End before the production was put on hold because of the coronavirus pandemic. Not wanting to let his big break pass him by, director Simon Evans must do his best to convince his doubtful actors to carry on rehearsals online.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike accidentally gets handcuffed to Freddy while attempting to eject the raccoons from the house. The two enemies have to find the key and free themselves ASAP.(n)

programme.desc: An unscrupulous mechanic cons Mr Bean out of his beloved car, but it's not long before he's found an alternative mode of transport which sends his spirits soaring. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson.(n)

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben bristles at Gwen's suggestion that she be his sidekick.(n)

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Love Island presenter Laura Whitmore and singers Beverley Knight and Jessie Ware join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner serve up their favourite summery salads, including a spiced lentil and salmon centrepiece, chicken caesar salad and a Vietnamese-inspired beef noodle salad. This is followed by macerated strawberries with shortbread for dessert.(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Baking expert Juliet Sear whips up a selection of treats in her studio kitchen, beginning with warm crumble tart, perfect for Sunday lunch, pasta pie and the ultimate chocolate fudge cake. She is also joined by award-winning baker Julie Jones for a lesson in pie art, and she visits a meeting of the Pudding Club in the Cotswolds.(n)

programme.title: Grow Your Own at Home with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan and the team reveal their individual garden makeovers, and Alan adds a final border of bee-attracting flowers to keep his vegetable patch pollinated. Frances Tophill's garden transformation is revealed - from tiny bare backyard to cosy kitchen-garden sanctuary. She completes it with a final addition of veg-filled hanging baskets and soil-conditioning climbers. In Yorkshire, Katie Rushworth reviews the six new mini veg gardens she's added, and finishes with one final one to complete her front garden.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The next two heat-winning families compete in the second semi-final of the knockout tournament, bringing them one step closer to the chance to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase's Anne Hegerty, aka The Governess, asks the questions.(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: In 1935, beleaguered Bournemouth widow Louisa Durrell decides to uproot her family and build a new life on the sun-drenched island of Corfu. However, with money problems and four unruly children to care for, even a new start might not be enough to banish her troubles. Period drama, starring Keeley Hawes, based on Gerald Durrell's trilogy of Corfu memoirs, which includes My Family and Other Animals.(n)

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective is distracted from a string of attacks on three wealthy farms by a series of anonymous phone calls from a man who claims a gang is trying to kill him. He and his trusty associates LaPointe and Janvier go in search of the caller, which leads them to the discovery of a body that has been dumped out of a car. Maigret is convinced the corpse is the man who phoned him - but his face has been mutilated, making it impossible to confirm his identity. Rowan Atkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When Toyah calls round to offer support, Leanne turns on her, insisting she has no idea how she's feeling. Sally's appalled to learn from Cathy how Geoff locked Yasmeen in his magic box. And when Michael reveals that he's got a date with Grace at the bistro, Aggie warns him not to get involved.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon amazes the others with her high IQ, and Dorien, under pressure to find a heroine for her next book, decides to push her toward a surprising new career. Meanwhile, Garth (played by Samuel James, replacing Matt Willis) is feeling despondent following his split from Australian Marcie, and he's proving a handful for mum Tracey. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star in the hit sitcom.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights. The first instalment focuses on the way that Coronation Street has always connected with its audience and made headlines by reflecting on pivotal storylines that captured the public's imagination, including the tram crash of 2010 and the Mike, Ken and Dierdre love triangle of 1983.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with two people whose lives have been transformed. Kathleen Fraser Jackson was reunited with her birth mother in Canada, and the programme follows her to Jamaica to meet her youngest sister Peta Gay for the first time. The second story has never been shown before and features twins who were separated as babies.(n)

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: Miller realises the evidence Jack handed over does not tally with her knowledge of Danny's possessions, while Olly and Karen work together on an article about the newsagent's past. However, the next morning when they read the paper, they discover the piece has been rewritten to make Jack look like a sex offender - and the police can offer little protection.(n)

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Liverpool
programme.desc: A look at Liverpool's four European Cup triumphs between 1977 and 1984, charting how manager Bob Paisley helped them to become a European superpower after his predecessor Bill Shankly had laid the foundations. With highlights of the finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved.(n)

programme.desc: Guest judge Jean-Christophe Novelli demonstrates how to cut sweet potatoes into thin matchsticks or allumettes before contestants must attempt their own. programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Mediterranean with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores an unusual hotel built into the side of a volcano on the Greek island of Santorini, before journeying to north Africa's Mediterranean coast, where he meets movie mogul Tarak Ben Ammar, the man who brought Star Wars to Tunisia. The final port of call on this leg of his trip is on the southern shores of France, where he meets the cowboys of the Camargue and witnesses a spectacular parade at the headquarters of the French Foreign Legion at Aubagne, near Marseille.(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: The film-maker investigates how the housing crisis has resulted in some vulnerable families agreeing to be moved by local councils - sometimes many miles away from loved ones - just to keep a roof over their heads. He meets a mother of three whose temporary accommodation across London has ended up being her home for three years so far, and also hears from families in Bradford who say that refusing an offer of accommodation 200 miles away would bring shocking repercussions.(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements.(n)

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together.(n)

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Agent Danny is given very special access to find out what a day in the life of a zookeeper is really like. He is tasked with looking after lots of different animals, including feeding the chimpanzees.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are trying to steal the president's jokebook. Magoo, thinking he is visiting a museum of sorcery, mistakes Weasel for a wizard.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a hoverboard. After a slip-up, the board ends up in the hands of Magoo, who thinks he has found a new ironing board.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz falls into the sewers, only to bump into Magoo, who thinks he's exploring the jungle. The latter mistakes Fizz for a lost child.(n)

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: When Tom suggests to his mom that Mike could be a show dog, she bursts out laughing. Mike is hurt and sets out to prove that he's show dog material.(n)

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike accidentally gets handcuffed to Freddy while attempting to eject the raccoons from the house. The two enemies have to find the key and free themselves ASAP.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An unscrupulous mechanic cons Mr Bean out of his beloved car, but it's not long before he's found an alternative mode of transport which sends his spirits soaring. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson.(n)

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben bristles at Gwen's suggestion that she be his sidekick.(n)

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Love Island presenter Laura Whitmore and singers Beverley Knight and Jessie Ware join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner serve up their favourite summery salads, including a spiced lentil and salmon centrepiece, chicken caesar salad and a Vietnamese-inspired beef noodle salad. This is followed by macerated strawberries with shortbread for dessert.(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Baking expert Juliet Sear whips up a selection of treats in her studio kitchen, beginning with warm crumble tart, perfect for Sunday lunch, pasta pie and the ultimate chocolate fudge cake. She is also joined by award-winning baker Julie Jones for a lesson in pie art, and she visits a meeting of the Pudding Club in the Cotswolds.(n)

programme.title: Grow Your Own at Home with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan and the team reveal their individual garden makeovers, and Alan adds a final border of bee-attracting flowers to keep his vegetable patch pollinated. Frances Tophill's garden transformation is revealed - from tiny bare backyard to cosy kitchen-garden sanctuary. She completes it with a final addition of veg-filled hanging baskets and soil-conditioning climbers. In Yorkshire, Katie Rushworth reviews the six new mini veg gardens she's added, and finishes with one final one to complete her front garden.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The next two heat-winning families compete in the second semi-final of the knockout tournament, bringing them one step closer to the chance to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase's Anne Hegerty, aka The Governess, asks the questions.(n)
