programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present coverage of racing from Sandown Park, including the 3.35 Coral Eclipse. Plus, three races from Haydock Park, including the Lancashire Oaks. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Mediterranean with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores an unusual hotel built into the side of a volcano on the Greek island of Santorini, before journeying to north Africa's Mediterranean coast, where he meets movie mogul Tarak Ben Ammar, the man who brought Star Wars to Tunisia. The final port of call on this leg of his trip is on the southern shores of France, where he meets the cowboys of the Camargue and witnesses a spectacular parade at the headquarters of the French Foreign Legion at Aubagne, near Marseille.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Forty-year-old mother of three Samantha Whyte turns to the programme for help as she searches for the Swiss father she has never met, having spent years dreaming about it. Raised by her single mother in Glasgow, Scotland, she was told the story of her parents' whirlwind romance in Zurich, and became desperate to know the man behind the myth. Meanwhile, Vanda James looks to put an end to a secret that has haunted her family for more than 50 years. When she was a child, a new-born baby was taken away at a few days old, and her mother never spoke about it. She discovered the baby was her brother and had been adopted, but her mother died before she had the courage to ask her about him.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Dogs
programme.desc: A countdown of the nation's 10 favourite breeds, based on a survey of 1,000 dog owners, discovering how the favourites have changed dramatically over the years. The survey also uncovers what makes people fall head over heels for a particular type of dog, with almost a quarter of all households in the UK accommodating one of man's best friends. The programme gets to meet the owners of some of Britain's most outstanding pooches, before revealing which breed emerges as top dog.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: In the final episode, Roz finally decides to confess to Lisa about what happened between her and Wayne, and together they decide to report it to the police. Meanwhile, Kate is rushed into hospital after Fergus finds her unconscious in the boathouse after a night of heavy drinking. When she is discharged from hospital, she convinces Guy to stay for Fergus' birthday party. Lisa becomes increasingly suspicious of Kate's role in Fergus' health, and Kate and Guy finally come to terms with the damage they have done to their family.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: Annie continues her determined investigation into the killer's identity, and reaches a breakthrough when she discovers the weapons that were used in the murders. Seeking help from Quigley, she is finally able to track down the culprit, and the two detectives put their lives on the line to put a stop to the slaughter. However, their relentless pursuit of justice leads them into a dangerous confrontation, as they stalk the armed murderer through the dark corridors of the area's hydro-electric dam.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. Rose, meanwhile, seems more concerned with persuading him to buy a wireless than what is happening to those around her - but then again, she's always been trapped in her own self-centred little world. In the kitchen, Mrs Patmore thinks she may have found a way to help Daisy cope with her studies, while Thomas' devious ways look set to scupper Baxter's friendship with Molesley. Finally, as Lord Merton continues to woo Isobel, Anna is reluctantly drawn into Mary's latest scheme.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates the disappearance of a teacher from a secretive public school, and begins to question who he can trust when a body is discovered. Thursday wrestles with the imminent closure of Cowley Station as he tries to find the vital evidence that will connect Eddie Nero to a series of unsolved murders, and assigns Fancy to track down the man who may be the key to cracking the case.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Mary's secret assignation with Lord Gillingham is almost exposed - until Violet saves the day. It seems that the battleaxe may have more reason than most to sympathise with her granddaughter's position when a face from her past reappears, hinting at a hidden past of her own. Meanwhile, Carson is dismissive of Mrs Patmore's latest problem, Branson receives a proposition about the estate's future and the circumstances of nasty valet Green's demise are scrutinised - again.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Forty-year-old mother of three Samantha Whyte turns to the programme for help as she searches for the Swiss father she has never met, having spent years dreaming about it. Raised by her single mother in Glasgow, Scotland, she was told the story of her parents' whirlwind romance in Zurich, and became desperate to know the man behind the myth. Meanwhile, Vanda James looks to put an end to a secret that has haunted her family for more than 50 years. When she was a child, a new-born baby was taken away at a few days old, and her mother never spoke about it. She discovered the baby was her brother and had been adopted, but her mother died before she had the courage to ask her about him.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Dogs
programme.desc: A countdown of the nation's 10 favourite breeds, based on a survey of 1,000 dog owners, discovering how the favourites have changed dramatically over the years. The survey also uncovers what makes people fall head over heels for a particular type of dog, with almost a quarter of all households in the UK accommodating one of man's best friends. The programme gets to meet the owners of some of Britain's most outstanding pooches, before revealing which breed emerges as top dog.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: In the final episode, Roz finally decides to confess to Lisa about what happened between her and Wayne, and together they decide to report it to the police. Meanwhile, Kate is rushed into hospital after Fergus finds her unconscious in the boathouse after a night of heavy drinking. When she is discharged from hospital, she convinces Guy to stay for Fergus' birthday party. Lisa becomes increasingly suspicious of Kate's role in Fergus' health, and Kate and Guy finally come to terms with the damage they have done to their family.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: Annie continues her determined investigation into the killer's identity, and reaches a breakthrough when she discovers the weapons that were used in the murders. Seeking help from Quigley, she is finally able to track down the culprit, and the two detectives put their lives on the line to put a stop to the slaughter. However, their relentless pursuit of justice leads them into a dangerous confrontation, as they stalk the armed murderer through the dark corridors of the area's hydro-electric dam.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. Rose, meanwhile, seems more concerned with persuading him to buy a wireless than what is happening to those around her - but then again, she's always been trapped in her own self-centred little world. In the kitchen, Mrs Patmore thinks she may have found a way to help Daisy cope with her studies, while Thomas' devious ways look set to scupper Baxter's friendship with Molesley. Finally, as Lord Merton continues to woo Isobel, Anna is reluctantly drawn into Mary's latest scheme.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates the disappearance of a teacher from a secretive public school, and begins to question who he can trust when a body is discovered. Thursday wrestles with the imminent closure of Cowley Station as he tries to find the vital evidence that will connect Eddie Nero to a series of unsolved murders, and assigns Fancy to track down the man who may be the key to cracking the case.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Mary's secret assignation with Lord Gillingham is almost exposed - until Violet saves the day. It seems that the battleaxe may have more reason than most to sympathise with her granddaughter's position when a face from her past reappears, hinting at a hidden past of her own. Meanwhile, Carson is dismissive of Mrs Patmore's latest problem, Branson receives a proposition about the estate's future and the circumstances of nasty valet Green's demise are scrutinised - again.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt.(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.desc: Jeff, Meg and Perry head to Vegas for one of the world's largest automotive trade show, where they check out a 1965 Malibu with a six-figure asking price and two hot rods - a 1951 Ford Shoebox and a 1953 Mercury. However, on arrival their claims to be on a trading mission are disbelieved and they are arrested and imprisoned by the Nameless Order within the Great Wall - just as it is about to be beset by giant, lizard-like monsters. As wave after wave of attacks and surprise tactics batter the Wall, Europeans and Chinese battle hoards of the alien creatures in a series of escalating conflicts leading to a final confrontation with the alien queen. Shahid and Riffat have worked all their lives planning for their retirement. They have built their own home and live off a comfortable pension. Kelly lives in a three-bedroom council house with her sons. She works as a cleaner and spends no money on herself, with everything going to her sons. How will both parties be affected by the swap?(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. The popularity of age-gap relationships among the super-rich, meeting six millionaire couples who are decades apart. Stories include that of 37-year-old Carolyn, who landed a job at 54-year-old multi-millionaire John's insurance company, little knowing that within a year she would be engaged to her boss and promoted to CEO of Mansfield Town football club. Meanwhile, DIY millionaire Craig had his life turned upside down when he won the UK's first Big Brother. programme.desc: Dan Snow and Kate Humble present a three-part guide to the critical aerial battle that changed the course of the Second World War, featuring personal stories of pilots, ground crews and members of the public. The first episode tracks the first skirmishes of the three-day battle,as the Luftwaffe began an all-out assault to rid Britain of air power prior to a land invasion. The first skirmishes were being tracked by a 19-year-old WAAF member in a secret London bunker, and her secret diaries provide fresh insight into the strategies behind the aerial combat.(n)

Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy.(n)

Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Andy Serkis, Jason Clarke and Gary Oldman.(n)

Mitch survives and becomes a master of unarmed combat before the US government consider using him for black ops. Action thriller, starring Dylan O'Brien, Michael Keaton and Sanaa Lathan.(n)

While attempting to overcome their differences, they have to try and track down the killer who has has been murdering teenagers in the town for several years. Thriller, set in 1980, starring Raúl Arévalo and Javier Gutiérrez. In Spanish.(n)

When the older man is appointed sheriff of a nearby town, he insists that his protege is made deputy - but soon discovers his loyalty is misplaced. Western, directed by Nicholas Ray and starring James Cagney, John Derek, Viveca Lindfors and Jean Hersholt.(n)

Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright, Dean Jagger, Judith Anderson, Alan Hale and John Rodney.(n)

Instead he challenges him to compete in a shooting competition to win a superb rifle, but when the murderer loses, he steals the coveted prize. Western, starring James Stewart, Shelley Winters, Dan Duryea and Stephen McNally.(n)

Court-martialled for his actions, he remains convinced his plan was sound and ignores the sentence to make a second attempt at destroying the emplacement. Second World War adventure, with Steve McQueen, James Coburn and Bobby Darin.(n)

He describes his childhood in India, living in the zoo run by his father, and how the family and their animals were forced to relocate to Canada when he was a teenager. However, during the sea voyage, the ship sank and he was left adrift in a small boat with a ferocious tiger. Ang Lee's adventure based on Yann Martel's novel, starring Suraj Sharma and Irrfan Khan.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Craig and Marie Harris welcome their guests to the Peaks Inn in Derbyshire, but a few of the guests are far from impressed upon arrival. When the hosts reveal ferret racing is their chosen activity, a joke causes Eileen to lose her temper, so Ian and Maggie decide to stage a silent protest.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hoteliers meet one last time for feedback, during which they will discover how much their rivals thought their service was worth, and which of them was the most successful. Maggie and Ian Dickson decide to tackle complaints from Eileen and Jannika Hopeakoski head-on, while Craig and Marie Harris prepare for a fight after telling German competitor Kristin Grantham her venue and welcome lacked some much-needed warmth.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness instructor Liza Macias hosts the first party in Warwickshire and hopes wooing her diners with a French menu and three hunky waiters will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sailor Steve Aston hosts the second dinner party in Warwickshire, hoping his nautical-themed evening will impress the women in the group and win him the £1,000 prize. But what will the ladies think when they discover the secrets of his 'love ship'?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Admin manager Emma Reddin hosts the third dinner party in Warwickshire, and hopes to secure the £1,000 prize by offering her guests pea and potato samosas and a selection of Indian curries, and organising Bollywood dancing.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PA Pinky Sidhu hosts the fourth dinner party in Warwickshire, hoping to educate her guests about the finer things in life with an evening of sophistication and pageantry. However, the diners quickly find fault with her starter and dessert, and there is a surprise winner of the beauty competition she arranged as entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Driving instructor Alison Firth hosts the final dinner party in Warwickshire, and hopes that her 1980s disco-themed night featuring a marshmallow competition will impress her guests enough to see her win the cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Revisited
programme.desc: An update on Kelly and Masoko Neville, who built an oak-framed, straw-baled hexagonal home in the Cambridgeshire Fens in 2005. The unusual two-storey house was built around a spiral staircase carved out of an 800-year-old tree trunk, the centrepiece of an interior that drew inspiration from JRR Tolkien's The Hobbit. Kevin McCloud visits the Nevilles to see if they have managed to find the idyllic, self-sufficient life they craved.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A look at some of the heart attack cases the West Midlands Ambulance Service paramedics are faced with, including a man whose heart has stopped beating. The crew manage to get it started again, only for it to stop once more.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With police numbers lower than they've been for decades, this edition focuses on the increased demands on front-line police in Northamptonshire. Call handlers are finding their work increasingly demoralising, while the squeeze on resources is not helped by the public's ever-broadening sense of what constitutes an emergency.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A look at some of the heart attack cases the West Midlands Ambulance Service paramedics are faced with, including a man whose heart has stopped beating. The crew manage to get it started again, only for it to stop once more.(n)

Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack, Dougal and Ted head off for a summer break at a caravan park. However, they immediately get on the wrong side of their fellow holiday-makers, and matters become even more dire when Father Noel Furlong turns up - with his hapless youth group in tow. Comedy, guest starring Graham Norton.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp shows viewers some quirky ways to dress a dining table, and reveals how to make a paper napkin rose and a cupcake bouquet. The presenter also demonstrates a recipe for chocolate bowls.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: A few years ago Kieran Alexander was involved in a serious motorbike accident that has given her, and partner Dave Dwyer, a new outlook on life - that it is for living and there is no time like the present. They've decided to leave their stressful lives in the UK behind and start again in the Azores in the Atlantic. With a budget of £100,000 to fund their new home, Ben Hillman shows them five properties on the main island of Sao Miguel.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien recycles some historic oak while Henry Cole comes unstuck when he tries to get to grips with an old Ford Escort.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, Dick and Angel harvest their pumpkins in the walled garden and organise a family wheelbarrow race. The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Mark and Gillian Webb, who plan to build a home in Hertfordshire in the style of a Georgian rectory. The couple were initially expecting to do as little of the work as possible themselves, but budget constraints mean they have to get their hands dirty on the project. The presenter hopes to inspire the pair by sending them to see a similar build in London.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. In the rooms, Donna Taylor from Jasmine House in Leicestershire makes a hair-raising discovery, while mother-and-daughter team Lesley and Chloe Priestly take a dislike to one of their competitors at dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove fix up vintage vehicles, with the opening edition seeing them track down a 1970s Uncle Bunt motorcycle to a shed in Preston.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A dachshund is brought in by owners Kelly and Daniel to see if anything can be done to repair a deformity in its foot, and Noel Fitzpatrick gives the one-year-old a lesson in doggy discipline along the way. A 10-month-old labrador needs a hip replacement before it can begin training as a medical-alert dog to help its owner with her rare health condition. An eight-year-old shih-tzu has the most extraordinary walk the vet has ever seen, but the operation to fix the dog's spine will be extremely risky, presenting the owners with an agonising decision.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: A look back at the transformation of Dick and Angel's 45-room chateau in France from abandoned ruin to sumptuous family home. The series begins by recalling the technical and engineering challenges they had to overcome to make it habitable for their young family. The couple remember their first winter together, when Dick's ingenious solutions were put to the test.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of self-made property developer Barry Surtees and his wife Julie as they try to build a four-storey mansion in Brighton, complete with pool, gym, artist's studio, fantasy bedroom and Japanese roof garden. The project is not without its difficulties as the cost of materials escalates, neighbours become unhappy about the house dwarfing the rest of the street, and Barry undergoes triple heart-bypass surgery.(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk: The Forgotten Heroes
programme.desc: The untold story of what happened after the Dunkirk evacuation, told by the men who were there and using recently declassified secret documents. Following the evacuation, tens of thousands of British troops were still left in France, fighting for their lives. The 51st Highland Division were ordered to fight on against the Hitler's war machine and now, almost 80 years later, the only remaining survivors of that Division tell their extraordinary story for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Home Bird Live
programme.desc: Stand-up show recorded in 2014 at Newcastle's Tyne Theatre & Opera House, in which the comedienne shares humorous insights into her life, including the need to put down some roots now she has cats. Plus, she offers advice on what to take away on a dirty weekend, how to teach a pensioner to swear and the correct number of meals to consume in a day.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon O'Brien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners.(n)

The guests are not impressed with having to pay extra for breakfast and Callum and Lauren are faced with robust feedback that leaves them wanting answers and thinking not everyone is being fair.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to Sue's B&B, which is run by Sue Lewis and her sister, Wendy Germann. Breakfast at the communal table has its awkward moments and Sue is under pressure, having never cooked for so many before. However, it's the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Park Hotel in Gloucestershire is up first as owners Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham want to get to the bottom of why Cameron didn't sleep soundly. Next, Sue Lewis from Sue's B&B seeks to discover the deasons for Callum and Lauren's low score for the cleanliness. When all issues are resolved, the winning establishment is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It
programme.desc: Bargain-hunting buddies Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove have been given the chance of a lifetime - to completely rebuild a classic 1930s Wolseley Hornet roadster.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and the team deal with two emergency cases in this edition. Lara the cat went missing after jumping into a removal van, but escaped, only to be hit by a car and then attacked by a fox. One of its rear legs is shattered and the vet has to perform reconstructive surgery to rebuild it. Chief, an eight-year-old Northern Inuit, dislocated its hip while chasing a fox. Noel thinks it needs hip-replacement surgery, but owner Nigel can't afford to pay for the operation.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The look back at the epic transformation of Dick Strawbridge and wife Angel's 45-room chateau continues, focusing on how Angel's creativity brought the chateau back to life, as walls were smashed, exquisitely designed rooms created and stunning bathroom suites installed.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Revisited
programme.desc: Cheryl Robson and Alex Reay were determined to rebuild their medieval cottage following a catastrophic fire, but were left owing £500,000 when they finished. Three years on, Kevin McCloud returns to the New Forest to see if they have found a way to repay their debts and whether they are now able to enjoy the good life.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Second World War history of Dover, visiting an underground base beneath the famous white cliffs, from where the Dunkirk evacuation was masterminded. She also talks gets a first-hand account of the town's wartime history from a 90-year-old local and experiences what life was like for members of the Auxiliary Territorial Service during the Battle of Britain. Plus, aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the sky to survey the challenges faced by Royal Navy vessels deployed in the English Channel.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his 900-mile road trip from Venice to Istanbul, sampling that array of gastronomic delights that lie on the route. In the penultimate episode Rick arrives in the Greek Peloponnese where he samples mouth-watering dishes such as rooster stew hilopites with mizithra cheese, and a home-made moussaka he claims is the best he have ever savoured. In his Symi island kitchen, Rick rolls up his sleeves to prepare galaktoboureko, a dessert of semolina custard in filo pastry, a favourite dish in Greece.(n)

programme.title: Eat Your Heart Out with Nick Helm
programme.desc: Nick samples some delights in Peckham at two imaginatively named eateries, tasting a bao bun at Mar Bao and some tapas at Miss Tapas.(n)

programme.title: Eat Your Heart Out with Nick Helm
programme.desc: Nick heads for rural Essex, where he mucks in as he grinds his own meat, plucks turkeys and hunts rabbits in a farm-to-fork experience.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, sampling the punch of its 730bhp V12 engine on the open roads of Scotland. Richard Hammond harnesses the power of motorsport on a quest to find the world's best taxi, and James May pays tribute to the former BBC Television Centre in White City, London, using a motorcyclist and a pair of parkour runners. Plus, the BAC Mono - an astonishingly fast car with just one seat - is put through its paces on the test track, and film director Ron Howard goes for a spin in the new Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three motoring enthusiasts head to Spain on a mission to help its struggling economy - but the convertible supercars they take are very expensive and anything but discreet. With Jeremy Clarkson in a McLaren MP4-12C, Richard Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 and James May behind the wheel of an Audi R8 V10, the trio encounter mountain passes, empty towns and an abandoned airport before creating their own street circuit in a deserted Madrid development. Plus, Sherlock star Benedict Cumberbatch sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey profiles two of Britain's greatest wartime leaders. He begins by revealing how Winston Churchill came to write a biography of his ancestor John, who led the nation against Louis XIV of France. Starkey also explores how preparations for the Second World War were affected by the prime minister's knowledge of his 17th-century forebear's achievements.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey explains how writing a biography of his ancestor John, the first Duke of Marlborough, changed the way Winston Churchill was seen by the public and politicians, and so paved the way for his return to power. The first volume, published in 1933, reinforced the perception of him as a man past his time. But it was the second instalment, depicting John Churchill's march to the Danube and victory at Blenheim, which began to mark out Winston as the only leading politician who truly knew war.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird

programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough highlights the curiosities that have led to accusations of forgery, but have ultimately helped assist the rethinking of evolution. When early explorers brought the first specimen of a duck-billed platypus to England in 1799, it was considered so bizarre it was deemed a hoax. Similarly, the midwife toad became the centre of a scientific storm in the 1920s that led to accusations of fakery.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey to Chichester, with the first leg beginning in Norwich. At the city's Norman castle he uncovers the Victorian public's gory fascination with crime and punishment and finds out how campaigners such as Elizabeth Fry worked to improve conditions for prisoners. Heading west to Thetford, he explores how the Victorian appetite for rabbits and their fur led to special train services to London, and ends in the Suffolk town of Brandon, where he tries his hand at flint-knapping.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Ipswich to Chelmsford, starting at an agricultural implements works with its own railway sidings. Continuing his journey south-west into Essex, Michael dredges for oysters off Mersea Island before taking the train to Witham, where he discovers a model farming establishment at Tiptree. His final destination is Chelmsford, home to the world's first purpose-built radio equipment factory, established by Guglielmo Marconi.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant returns for more train journeys through some of the world's most challenging terrain. He heads to the Arctic for a trip along the Alaska Railroad, but as he arrives in the port of Seward, it's not a great start as there are no trains running, so he ends up hitching a ride with a track inspector's car. He continues his journey on a freight train heading for Anchorage, Alaska's biggest city, and finally heads north on the Aurora passenger train to the town of Healy. Along the way he checks out the tiny village of Eklutna, which has great importance for the indigenous inhabitants, and goes for an intrepid fishing trip on a thawing frozen lake.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough encounters two species with wrinkly skin, where nature has tinkered with the ageing process to different effect. The mole rat retains its looks while growing old and the elephant looks old from birth, yet both creatures live relatively long lives.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attend questions why spirals are so widespread in nature. He gleans some answers by examining the narwhal's tusk and the spiral shells found on snails and ammonites, as he continues to discover nature's evolutionary anomalies.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

Shows under scrutiny include 127 Hours, Planet Earth II, Cape Fear, This Morning on public toilets, EastEnders, Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad, and BBC news reports on beaches and travel.(n)

programme.title: Touching the Void (2003)
programme.desc: Director Kevin Macdonald's acclaimed, hugely powerful documentary recreating the terrifying struggle for survival faced by mountaineers Joe Simpson and Simon Yates, who braved the Peruvian Andes. During the descent, one of the men fell, breaking his leg and forcing his friend to leave him - but remarkably he beat all the odds to survive. The story is told in the climbers' own words and features dramatised reconstructions of their ordeal.(n)

Phil is looking for the perfect semi-rural spot for vets Sam and Andy, and the search becomes a game of compromise between the right house and right location.(n)

programme.title: The School That Tried to End Racism
programme.desc: Second of the two-part documentary about a pioneering British school giving 11 and 12-year-old pupils a series of activities designed to uncover and eradicate hidden racial biases. The students explore the notion of white privilege. A 12-year-old white British student is shocked to hear her black classmates' experiences of racism, while an 11-year-old girl is keen to create an Asian and bi-racial work group. At the end of the course, the pupils are tested again for racial bias, to see if anything has changed.(n)

programme.title: How to Build

programme.title: The Supervet

Plus, a man comes all the way from France with his Labrador cross, which requires high-end surgery for elbow dysplasia.(n)

programme.title: Big Ben: Saving the World's Most Famous Clock
programme.desc: Documentary about restoration work on the iconic clock tower, which will see the bells silenced for the next four years. Anna Keay, Director of Landmark Trust, goes behind the scenes on this massive project, and talks to Parliament's principal architect and the three clockmakers who have the challenging task of taking the gigantic mechanism apart for the first time in nearly 160 years. The programme also examines the history of the clock tower, revealing how it was almost never built in the first place.(n)

programme.title: Big Ben: Saving the World's Most Famous Clock
programme.desc: Anna Keay, Director of the Landmark Trust, presents the second in an occasional series going behind the scenes of the clock tower's ongoing restoration, meeting key personnel working to maintain the iconic tower, clock and the great bell itself. This episode features the foundry owner in Halifax who has been charged with making the new cast ironwork for the clock tower and explores how modern technology is revealing long-buried secrets in the tower's original design - including a mystery at the very pinnacle of the tower.(n)

Finally, Emma and Simon host with a traditional Jersey menu in an opulent country house setting, hoping to secure the cash prize of £1,000.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple with a gite in France find their pool infested with algae, a family running a campsite find themselves with no hot water, and money troubles threaten holiday lets in Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Babak and Alison from Barnsley are planning a permanent move to Almeria in southern Spain. The couple want to head inland and focus their search around the host of pretty towns and villages this mountainous region has to offer. Danni Menzies has five options within their maximum budget of £145,000, but will any of them match the pair's expectations of a dream home?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Julie Okine and Simon Sheriff, a pair of barristers in London, aim to fulfil a long-held dream of buying a holiday home in Spain, after Julie inherited some money from her mother. With a cash budget of £100,000 in mind, Laura Hamilton shows them five very different properties along the eastern Costa del Sol.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second venue of the week is the Hotel Barge Waternimf in Ely, Cambridgeshire, which has an open plan kitchen leaving the hosts exposed to everything the guests have to say.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Copywriter Will serves up a vegetarian meal on the second evening in Brighton, then takes his guests into the living room for a rendition of his own dance track.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A caller to his radio show gives Frasier the impression Niles's wife Maris might be having an affair with her fencing instructor. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In south London, Catherine and Julian kick-off the contest hoping to secure the £1,000 prize with an evening of simple home cooking. Night two sees a display of underplayed fine-dining laid on by Lucie and Alex, while the evening night offers the opportunity for entertainers Joe and Sheila to shine.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Holiday hosts in France are reviewed by a travel website, renovation work pushes a project in Spain to breaking point, and cycling tour operators struggle with logistics.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired civil servant Marie can't wait to leave the London rat race and make a permanent move to Poitou Charentes, France, with the new additions to her family - four adopted cats. As son Alex plans to visit mum as much as he can, he's come along too. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties within Marie's £110,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the luxury car manufacturer, with unrestricted access to the workers on the factory floor as they transform raw materials into the finished product. The first episode follows the construction of a new £170,000 Continental GT from the bolts to the finished car, paying a visit to the company's designers and the craftsmen in the wood shop.(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play, demonstrating how big dreams can be achieved in small and affordable places. In the third episode, George meets a couple crafting a magical den for their toddler with a helping hand from a grandparent, while an aircraft technician turns a jet engine's housing into a unique camper. Elsewhere in Chile, George discovers some lip-smacking designs in a winery.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud reveals his top five properties from the show, revisiting the buildings and people who have most inspired him over the years. From concrete-vaulted glass-fronted pavilions and heroic restoration projects, to handcrafted hobbit houses made of hedgerow, Kevin reflects on what it takes to build a truly great Grand Designs project.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager with anorexia nervosa is rushed into the paediatrics emergency department with dangerously low blood pressure. Her mother reflects on her daughter's lifelong struggle with her eating disorder, and the pain she feels at being unable to protect her child. A 66-year-old man who fell from a great height onto a concrete surface is brought in to St George's, and doctors fear he has sustained organ damage and internal bleeding. His daughter talks about their close relationship and the sacrifices her father has made over the years.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

The duo also chase £5,000 for an unpaid parking fine, which comes as a shock to the debtor, who has clearly hit hard times.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the ups and downs of life for people on benefits in the UK, beginning with a visit to Liverpool to look at the effect of the bedroom tax. Lorna was living in a three-bedroom housing association home with her children, but when they moved out she was hit with a weekly £40 bedroom tax, which she has to pay out of her £72.40 employment and support allowance. Keith and Marie had been in their family home for 35 years, but after refusing to pay an extra £24 they were rehoused in a one-bedroom flat and forbidden from taking any of their animals with them.(n)

programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tries to unravel the mysterious theft of a painting, and also finds herself investigating the death of a friend's daughter. Starring Angela Lansbury, Norman Lloyd and Cliff Gorman.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Friend in Deed (1974)
programme.desc: A Californian police commissioner helps a rich, influential friend to conceal his complicity in murdering his own wife - but reckons without detective Columbo's timely intervention and his dogged determination to bring the truth to light. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Richard Kiley and Rosemary Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Grand Deceptions (1989)
programme.desc: An army colonel who embezzled money from his superior officer to fund arms sales becomes the target of a blackmail attempt when his crimes are discovered. The crooked officer kills the extortionist and makes it look like an accident, but the crumpled cop is soon on his case. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Foxworth and Janet Elber.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder, Smoke and Shadows (1989)
programme.desc: An ambitious Hollywood director is visited by an old friend, who threatens to expose evidence of his involvement in the death of an actor. The film-maker resorts to murder to keep his dark secret safe, only to end up on the receiving end of Columbo's distinctive brand of interrogation. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Suitable for Framing (1971)
programme.desc: A millionaire art collector is thought to have been killed as the result of a random burglary. However, Columbo is convinced the dead man's nephew committed the crime to avoid being written out of the will, and embarks on a mission to ensnare his suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ross Martin and Don Ameche.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Crucial Game (1972)
programme.desc: The owner of an American football team is found dead in a swimming pool, and his drowning is regarded as an accident. The down-at-heel detective is convinced that foul play was involved and sets out to prove his case - but his chief suspect has a cast-iron alibi that places him miles away from the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Robert Culp.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A famous crime writer who used to date Captain Ross asks him to look into the disappearance of her much younger husband. Goren and Eames trace the man's movements via his car's GPS tracking system and suspect he was the victim of a carjacking. Further inquires lead them to believe he had staged the crime to run off with his lover, who is subsequently reported missing by her parents. Guest starring Brenda Strong (Desperate Housewives) and Eric Roberts (Heroes).(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson hopes justice will be served as child abductor Lewis Hodda goes on trial. However, when a plea deal is rejected, Barba must rely on decades-old evidence to make his case, and faces off against former SVU colleague Dr Huang. Crime drama, guest starring Tom Sizemore.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A high-school student succumbs to the flirtations of an attractive female teacher, and begins to plan a clandestine encounter. However, he quickly finds himself in a far more terrifying situation. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The daughter of a man convicted for rape and incest seeks to change the testimony she gave and bring about her father's release - but the judge refuses to reopen the case.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An eight-year-old boy is snatched from his school, and when Amaro examines surveillance footage of the incident, it becomes clear the child knew his kidnapper.(n)

Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a gay man beaten to death during the abduction of his adopted infant son. At first they suspect the victim's wealthy partner may be complicit in the crime - until information about the child's birth mother and homophobic biological father prompts them to pursue a different line of inquiry.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant loan officer is found murdered in the boot of her car and the trail leads to drugs and a professional basketball player, whose ties to the victim appear to extend beyond the financial deal she approved for him. Detective drama, with Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy discovers two suicide victims were participating in clinical trials of an experimental antidepressant, prompting him to charge the drug company's CEO with second-degree murder. In court the defence argues all medical research carries an element of risk and the judge rules prosecution evidence inadmissible, leaving the outcome on a knife-edge. Sam Waterston and Dennis Farina star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A wealthy widow and her daughter are implicated in the death of a hitman out on parole - and it's up to the squad to find out whether the pair hired the victim to kill a rich relative. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Called to investigate, Tony and McGee try to determine whether the intended target was the 1980s rocker headlining the act or if the attack was part of a larger scheme against the military. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the suspicious death of a reality TV contestant. However, the incriminating tape cannot be found. Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill star.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the death of Karen Hall, a staff member in the State Attorney General's Office, whose body has been found in the Hudson River - and her boss Frank Conroy quickly becomes a suspect. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.desc: A convicted child murderer is struck and left for dead by a motorist just weeks after his prison release, but Fontana and Green are initially unenthusiastic about finding out who ran him over. It is not until the killer dies from his injuries that they investigate inside the prison where he served his sentence - and uncover evidence that leads McCoy to make a startling discovery. Starring Dennis Farina.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of an apartment block resident prompts Briscoe and Green to investigate her long-standing battle with the landlord in the hope of finding a credible suspect. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the shooting of a singer's wife, questioning the man's manager - who proves to have formerly owned the murder weapon. Meanwhile, an unscrupulous defence lawyer embarks on a smear campaign against the victim. Gary Busey guest stars.(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Gibbs joins forces with the FBI when the body of a congressman and former agent washes ashore in New Orleans. Together they try to determine if the murder could be linked to a well-known case the victim had previously worked on during his career. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette.(n)

Starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates when Libby Parker is followed home by a gang of teenagers, and raped in the courtyard of her apartment block. Hundreds of windows overlooked the scene of the crime, but not one of the residents who witnessed the attack called the police. The prosecutors face an even more difficult dilemma when it emerges none of Libby's neighbours are willing to make a statement either. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Notorious serial killer Gregory Williams Yeats concocts an elaborate plan to free himself from incarceration by co-operating with one-time nemesis Dr Carl Rudnick. They follow each of the sales at auction before talking to the buyers about their plans for the buildings. After several months the team returns to review the progress.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from London with a generous budget of £800,000, search for a rural retreat in West Sussex. While in the area, the presenter also visits a local wetlands centre to see some of the world's rarest wildfowl.(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: The sheriffs help a woman who is owed wages from a north London college, while Darryl and Mark go after a company that promised a pensioner a new driveway, and Marc and Tony come to the aid of a man who lost his life savings on a tooth-whitening franchise. Plus, Pete heads to Blackpool airport in search of a sizeable debt.(n)

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The city is hit by torrential rain and flash-flooding, and the ASPCA needs to act quickly to save a herd of horses from drowning.(n)

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Medical technician Stacie Dabolt is called upon to rescue two dogs trapped in the heat of the midday sun.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charlie Ross and Natasha Raskin compete to find the best antiques in the city of Portsmouth, before heading to an auction in the Hampshire village of Swanmore to see how much money they can make.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Margie Cooper scour Scarborough for antiques to take to an auction in Rotherham, where Paul comes across a historical item from the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Margie Cooper search Chesterfield, and while he uncovers an unusual First World War item, she hopes a gruesome find will attract bids at the auction in Leicester.(n)

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Seeing an untapped source of rare items, Drew Pritchard tours Catalonia looking for some chic Spanish antiques. In Peralada, he is blown away by a 17th-century house full of treasures.(n)

programme.title: Born Mucky: Life on the Farm
programme.desc: Tom seeks expert help to save one of his ancient hedgerows. Meanwhile, Ian has a loose pig on his hands when one of them escapes during weigh-in and Ally runs out of storage.(n)

programme.title: Paranormal Lockdown UK
programme.desc: Nick Groff and Katrina Weidman immerse themselves in the vast Drakelow Tunnels, a former top secret underground military complex beneath Kingsford Country Park north of Kidderminster, Worcestershire. They investigate the stories of shadowed figures and dark entities that lurk underground.(n)

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Renowned dog trainer Cesar Millan tries to tame a vicious chihuahua that attacks anyone who approaches its owner. Plus, a pet that refuses to walk on shiny surfaces, such as lino flooring and wet streets, after an accident involving a glass door.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Baby woolly monkey Paulo meets his new family for the first time, while chimpanzee Eddi has fun with his young brother Bart. Pregnant orang-utan Amy gets to grips with a breast pump, and emotions run high when a marmoset arrives at the park.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Mad Max meets his new housemates, and chimps Ben and Pip get a shock when they have another encounter with alpha-male Hananya. Piquita is the centre of attention in the woolly monkey enclosure, while Bart enjoys honey for the first time in Paddy's chimp group.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Pregnant woolly monkey Piquita approaches her due date at Dorset's Monkey World, while capuchin Sean from Chile demonstrates his grooming skills and ruffed lemur Blue's life hangs in the balance.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A baby woolly monkey is tempted into the outside world by the prospect of some tasty bugs. Meanwhile, the capuchins enjoy a feast of onions, and the keepers plan a radical scheme that will allow a third meeting between young chimp Ben and alpha male Hananya.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The female capuchins fight, Tia needs surgery, and orangutan Amy enters the late stages of pregnancy. Elsewhere, there is a new addition to the play area in the chimp nursery, and Alison visits Paulo - the baby woolly monkey she has successfully hand-reared - for the final time.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Animal director Jeremy is concerned about Ben the chimp, who has a nasty foot wound, and there is an update on Paddy and his baby Bart. Gibbon Tien undergoes a transformation and keeper Holly checks up on woolly monkey Xuzy.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: An infection threatens to sweep through the chimpanzees, prompting the keepers to begin urgent action to save their lives. Meanwhile, the overweight orang-utans continue with their diet and the lemurs are introduced to the stump-tailed macaques before they move in together.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Young chimp Ben's mysterious bacterial infection causes him to lose a toe, while another primate in Hananya's house is struck down by the same illness. Capuchin TJ settles in well with the ladies and his South American housemates learn how to forage for food. Members of staff hide hard-boiled eggs for the capuchins to find and animal director Jeremy has a run-in with female orangutan Lingga.(n)

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee travels to Central and South America, Africa and Asia to reveal the stories behind the origins of each of the world's primate species, coming face-to-face with some of the planet's most charismatic and mischievous monkeys.(n)

programme.title: Frontline Police
programme.desc: Rav Wilding accompanies Essex police on a drugs raid, but the operation turns into a chase when the suspect escapes through the back door. Paramedics rush to Epping Forest to help an unconscious man, an emergency unit struggles to get a woman to the police station, and a routine traffic stop catches a motorist who is three times over the drink-drive limit. Elsewhere, officers search for a man who has been accused of threatening his former girlfriend - and find him in a very unusual place.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: Fred Dinenage offers an insight into the life of Lord Lucan, suspected of murdering his children's nanny, Sandra Rivett, in November 1974. The peer subsequently disappeared and has never been seen since, though unsubstantiated rumours suggest he died in Africa in 2004.(n)

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: A look into the death of church organist Alan Greaves, who was attacked on his way to midnight mass in Sheffield in 2012.(n)

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: A Special Constable gets an unpleasant introduction to frontline policing. Officers respond to an urgent call in Newcastle city centre.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Investigating how university student Howard Marks became one of the world's biggest cannabis traffickers and how his vast empire eventually began to unravel.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story behind Ben Affleck's Academy Award-winning film Argo as told by retired CIA officer Antonio Mendez who freed American diplomats from Iran by pretending to be a film producer. Including interviews with two of the embassy staff whose lives he saved, Cora Amburn-Lijek and Mark Lijek.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of brothers-in-law Jim PapRocki and Chris Jury, who tried to smuggle marijuana into the US from Mexico by plane only to end up stranded miles from the border when their engine overheated.(n)

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Wesley is in trouble when he gets involved in a desperate cover-up after his Starfleet Academy squadron suffers a fatal collision. Sci-fi adventure, guest starring Robert Duncan McNeill, with Wil Wheaton.(n)

programme.title: Hunt For The Shoe Rapist - Nowhere To Hide
programme.desc: Documentary examining how police caught a man in Rotherham who raped six women and stole their shoes.(n)

programme.title: Killer Colleagues

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The team searches for a missing 12-year-old girl, a case made more urgent by the discovery that she was to donate a kidney to her brother, for whom time is running out, and the investigation leads Brass and Grissom to a registered sex offender. programme.title: Road Wars

David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond.(n)

Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson.(n)

programme.desc: A detective investigates a series of murders, while the mysterious killer pursues an actress who may be able to identify him. Thriller, starring Edward Underdown and Maxwell Reed.(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow embarks on a grime-filled journey through the histories of three of the world's leading modern cities. He begins with London, using CGI footage and re-enactments to experience life in the capital in the 14th century. He spends a night as a medieval muck-raker, and tries on a pair of wooden platform shoes designed to elevate the wearer above the filth that covered the streets.(n)

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on the case of Jake and Victoria Ward, who were investigated by police and social services when they were unable to explain a serious injury to their three-month-old son. It took the couple two years to clear their names and a further three to win the right to speak openly about what happened to them. Darragh MacIntyre explores their fight to open up the closed world of the family courts.(n)

programme.title: UCMMA
programme.desc: Dave O'Donnell, CJ and Grant Waterman present MMA action from Troxy London.(n)

programme.desc: A man who fled his home town to escape his abusive father decides to return once he receives word that the old man is dead. However, he faces a bitter reunion with the brother, who stayed behind to care for their father in his last days, and his ex-girlfriend, whom he abandoned with no explanation.
